LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DEEM INC.'S (FKA REARDEN COMMERCE INC.) DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION ACCORDING TO THE CONTRACT'S LEGAL INTENDMENT AND EFFECT. PARAGRAPH 8 OF THE COMPLAINT PLEADS THE TERMS OF THE CONTRACT, PARAGRAPH 9 PLEADS PLAINTIFF'S PERFORMANCE, AND PARAGRAPHS 7 AND 11 ALLEGE THAT THE CONTRACT IS WRITTEN. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS DEFENDANT ACCESS PUBLIC RELATIONS LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO APRIL 21, 2014. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT ACCESS PUBLIC RELATIONS LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. OVERRULED. THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT ("FACC") STATES THAT "ACCESS MADE REPRESENTATIONS THAT IT POSSESSED THE EXPERIENCE, QUALIFICATIONS, AND PERSONNEL NECESSARY TO PRODUCE THE SERVICES AND WORK PRODUCT ACCORDING TO DEEM'S SPECIFICATIONS." (FACC, 12.) AS PLED, THIS STATEMENT IS NOT MERELY ADVERTISING PUFFERY. RATHER, CROSS-DEFENDANT AFFIRMATIVELY REPRESENTED THAT IT HAD THE ABILITY TO PERFORM A SPECIFIC JOB. PULVERS V. KAISER FOUNDATION HEALTH PLAN (1979) 99 CAL. APP. 3D 560, 564, RELIED ON BY MOVING PARTY, IS INAPPOSITE. IN THAT CASE, THE DOCTOR'S STATEMENT THAT HE WOULD PROVIDE A "GOOD RESULT" WAS IN THE CONTEXT OF A BREACH OF WARRANTY ACTION, NOT FRAUD. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

Off Calendar Nov-20-2014 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-07-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RICHARD MOONEY OF THE RIMON LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 539-0443 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. KELLY BORUSZEWSKI OF WEISBERG & MILLER, TEL. NO. 296-7070 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DEEM INC.'S (FKA REARDEN COMMERCE INC.) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication - The motion for summary judgment is denied in its entirety. Deem failed to maintain its initial summary judgment burden of production with regard to Access's complaint based on Access's discovery responses. Those responses are unverified and tantamount to no response at all. See Appleton v. Sup. Crt., (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636; Food 4 Less Supermarkets, Inc. v. Sup. Crt., (1995) 40 Cal.App.4th 651, 657. Deem did, however, shift the burden based on the declaration of Kristina Taylor. Ms. Taylor declares that Access failed to perform the contract. (Taylor dec., par. 11). In opposition, the declaration of Brian Regan creates a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Access performed the contract. The court overrules Deem's objection to that declaration. The motion is denied as to Deem's cross-complaint. First, the unverified discovery responses that Deem relies upon does not shift the burden as explained above. Second, Deem at most has shown that Access does not have facts to support its defenses. Form Interrogatory 15.1 asks for facts regarding Access's defenses. To maintain its initial summary judgment/summary adjudication burden on the cross-complaint, Deem needs to present evidence to establish the elements of each of its causes of action. See CCP 437c(p)(1). Deem failed to do so in this motion.
ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Richard Mooney, Esq. of Rimon PC, Tel. 800.930.7271. x 246, for cross defendant/plaintiff and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT ACCESS PUBLIC RELATIONS LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is Overruled. The First Amended Cross-Complaint ("FACC") states that "Access made representations that it possessed the experience, qualifications, and personnel necessary to produce the services and work product according to Deem's specifications." (FACC, 12.) As pled, this statement is not merely advertising puffery. Rather, Cross-Defendant affirmatively represented that it had the ability to perform a specific job. Pulvers v. Kaiser Foundation Health Plan (1979) 99 Cal. App. 3d 560, 564, relied on by moving party, is inapposite. In that case, the doctor's statement that he would provide a "good result" was in the context of a breach of warranty action, not fraud. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-05-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DEEM INC.'S (FKA REARDEN COMMERCE INC.) DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently pleads the breach of contract cause of action according to the contract's legal intendment and effect. Paragraph 8 of the complaint pleads the terms of the contract, paragraph 9 pleads plaintiff's performance, and paragraphs 7 and 11 allege that the contract is written. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
